def calculate_cost_and_count(nums, start)
    n = nums.length
    cost = 0
    count = 0
    
    i = start
    while i <= n - 2
        # Required height H_i must be strictly greater than both neighbors.
        # H_i = max(nums[i-1], nums[i+1]) + 1
        h_i = [nums[i-1], nums[i+1]].max + 1
        
        # The target value B_i must be at least nums[i] and at least H_i.
        # B_i = max(nums[i], H_i)
        b_i = [nums[i], h_i].max
        
        # Cost contribution: B_i - nums[i]
        cost += b_i - nums[i]
        count += 1
        
        i += 2
    end
    
    { cost: cost, count: count }
end

def solve(nums)
    n = nums.length
    
    if n < 3
        return 0
    end
    
    # Test starting at 1 (S1: {1, 3, 5, ...})
    result1 = calculate_cost_and_count(nums, 1)
    
    min_cost = result1[:cost]
    max_count = result1[:count]

    # Test starting at 2 (S2: {2, 4, 6, ...})
    if n >= 4
        result2 = calculate_cost_and_count(nums, 2)
        
        if result2[:count] > max_count
            # S2 achieves a strictly higher count
            max_count = result2[:count]
            min_cost = result2[:cost]
        elsif result2[:count] == max_count
            # S2 achieves the same maximal count, minimize cost
            min_cost = [min_cost, result2[:cost]].min
        end
    end
    
    return min_cost
end

function calculateCostAndCount(nums, start) {
    const N = nums.length;

    let count = 0;

        // Cost contribution
        cost += B_i - nums[i];
        count++;
    }
    
    return { cost: cost, count: count };

/**
 * Calculates the minimum cost required to make all indices in the sequence starting at `start`
 * (and incrementing by 2) into local maxima, assuming all non-peak neighbors remain at original values.
 * @param {number[]} nums
 * @param {number} start 1 or 2
 * @returns {{cost: number, count: number}}
 */
function calculateCostAndCount(nums, start) {
    const N = nums.length;
    let cost = 0;
    let count = 0;
    
    // Indices are 1 to N-2 inclusive.
    for (let i = start; i <= N - 2; i += 2) {
        // i is a peak candidate
        
        // Required height H_i = max(nums[i-1], nums[i+1]) + 1
        const H_i = Math.max(nums[i - 1], nums[i + 1]) + 1;
        
        // Target value B_i = max(nums[i], H_i)
        const B_i = Math.max(nums[i], H_i);
        
        // Cost contribution
        cost += B_i - nums[i];
        count++;
    }
    
    return { cost: cost, count: count };
}

function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    if (N < 3) {
        return 0;
    }
    
    // Check starting at 1 (S1)
    const result1 = calculateCostAndCount(nums, 1);
    
    let minCost = Infinity;
    let maxCount = 0;
    
    // S1 always exists and has a certain count.
    maxCount = result1.count;
    minCost = result1.cost;

    // Check starting at 2 (S2)
    // S2 is only possible if 2 <= N - 2, i.e., N >= 4.
    if (N >= 4) {
        const result2 = calculateCostAndCount(nums, 2);
        
        if (result2.count > maxCount) {
            // S2 achieves a strictly higher count
            maxCount = result2.count;
            minCost = result2.cost;
        } else if (result2.count === maxCount) {
            // S2 achieves the same maximal count, minimize cost
            minCost = Math.min(minCost, result2.cost);
        }
    }
    
    return minCost;
}
```

```javascript
/**
 * Calculates the minimum cost required to make all indices in the sequence starting at `start`
 * (and incrementing by 2) into local maxima, assuming all non-peak neighbors remain at original values.
 * @param {number[]} nums
 * @param {number} start 1 or 2
 * @returns {{cost: number, count: number}}
 */
function calculateCostAndCount(nums, start) {
    const N = nums.length;
    let cost = 0;
    let count = 0;
    
    // Indices are 1 to N-2 inclusive.
    for (let i = start; i <= N - 2; i += 2) {
        // i is a peak candidate
        
        // Required height H_i = max(nums[i-1], nums[i+1]) + 1
        const H_i = Math.max(nums[i - 1], nums[i + 1]) + 1;
        
        // Target value B_i = max(nums[i], H_i)
        const B_i = Math.max(nums[i], H_i);
        
        // Cost contribution
        cost += B_i - nums[i];
        count++;
    }
    
    return { cost: cost, count: count };
}

function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    if (N < 3) {
        return 0;
    }
    
    // Check starting at 1 (S1)
    const result1 = calculateCostAndCount(nums, 1);
    
    let minCost = Infinity;
    let maxCount = 0;
    
    // S1 always exists and has a certain count.
    maxCount = result1.count;
    minCost = result1.cost;

    // Check starting at 2 (S2)
    // S2 is only possible if 2 <= N - 2, i.e., N >= 4.
    if (N >= 4) {
        const result2 = calculateCostAndCount(nums, 2);
        
        if (result2.count > maxCount) {
            // S2 achieves a strictly higher count
            maxCount = result2.count;
            minCost = result2.cost;
        } else if (result2.count === maxCount) {
            // S2 achieves the same maximal count, minimize cost
            minCost = Math.min(minCost, result2.cost);
        }
    }
    
    return minCost;
}
```
